title: HealthDataStore + deterministic NumericsQueryLayer
description: Implement the data + numerics layer that keeps arithmetic out of the LLM. HealthDataStore is a single generic events table (better-sqlite3 sync, mirroring FactStore) with INSERT OR IGNORE on a deterministic SHA-256 id of (metric|tStart|source|value); it exposes upsertObservations (returns NEW-row count), getObservations, getLabSeries, getBaseline/putBaseline, getPreference, close. NumericsQueryLayer exposes ONLY the closed NumericPrimitive whitelist (mean|min|max|latest|delta|slope|percentile|zscore) via getMetric plus getLabTrend; sampleCount 0 => value null (abstain). No eval, no codegen, no subprocess. The LLM never performs arithmetic.

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript strict-mode typecheck passes with zero errors (npm run typecheck).
All tests pass including new HealthDataStore + NumericsQueryLayer tests (using temp dirs, no fs mocks); the full pre-existing suite stays green.

HealthDataStore.upsertObservations dedupes via INSERT OR IGNORE on the deterministic SHA-256 id and returns the count of NEW rows only: a test inserts N rows then re-inserts the same N and asserts the second call returns 0 while getObservations still returns N.
Each of the 8 NumericPrimitives (mean,min,max,latest,delta,slope,percentile,zscore) returns the correct value on a known fixture series (asserted against hand-computed expected values).
getMetric over an empty window returns NumericResult { value: null, sampleCount: 0 } (abstain) and does NOT throw; getLabTrend on an unknown biomarker abstains similarly.

NumericsQueryLayer reads the per-row unit and refuses (does not silently mix) aggregation across heterogeneous units for the same metric — a test with mixed units asserts a refusal/abstain rather than a blended number.
No eval, no Function constructor, no code generation, and no child_process/execa appears anywhere in the numerics code (grep/test over src/medical numerics files returns nothing).

nonGoals: Do not implement ingestion / SAX parsing (Sprint 5) — tests insert observations directly via upsertObservations.
Do not implement EgressGuard, medications, or the full SOP wiring (Sprint 6).
Do not add numeric primitives beyond the 8 whitelisted ones.
Do not store medications in HealthDataStore (medications are FactStore value-of-record — Sprint 6 / ADR-7).
stopConditions: Dedup + NEW-row count correct; all 8 primitives correct; empty window abstains; cross-unit refused; no eval/codegen/subprocess
definitionOfDone: src/medical/ gains HealthDataStore (better-sqlite3 sync, generic single events table + a lab_results table or shared table, deterministic SHA-256 id, INSERT OR IGNORE, the full accessor surface) and NumericsQueryLayer (closed NumericPrimitive whitelist + getLabTrend, sampleCount-0 abstain, cross-unit refusal). No eval/codegen/subprocess. Build, typecheck, and all tests pass.
assumptions: HealthObservation + LabResult + NumericResult shapes per the spec data model. The deterministic id is SHA-256 of `${metric}|${tStart}|${source}|${value}` (mirroring FactStore factId at src/state/facts.ts:58-69).
better-sqlite3 is already a dependency (used by FactStore at src/state/facts.ts); HealthDataStore uses the same sync API and a per-store db file path passed in (temp dir in tests).

slope is a simple linear least-squares slope over (t,value); percentile uses a documented method (e.g. nearest-rank or linear interpolation — pick one and test it); zscore is (latest - mean)/stddev. Document the chosen method in code.
Timestamps (tStart) are ISO string parameters on the observations; the store never reads the clock.
generatorNotes:
Add src/medical/health-store.ts (HealthDataStore) and src/medical/numerics.ts (NumericsQueryLayer), collocated *.test.ts.

HealthDataStore: model on FactStore (src/state/facts.ts) — same better-sqlite3 sync pattern. Single generic observations table: columns (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, metric TEXT, value REAL, unit TEXT, t_start TEXT, t_end TEXT, source TEXT). A separate lab_results table (id, biomarker, value, unit, collected_at, ref_low, ref_high) OR a typed view — keep labs distinct from observations. id = sha256(`${metric}|${tStart}|${source}|${value}`) via node:crypto (see facts.ts:58-69 for the exact factId helper to mirror). upsertObservations uses `INSERT OR IGNORE` in a transaction and returns db.changes summed (count of NEW rows). getObservations(metric, fromIso, toIso) filters by metric and t_start range, ordered by t_start. getBaseline/putBaseline + getPreference back a small key/value table. close() closes the db. All sync. Use node:fs/promises only for mkdir of the parent dir if needed.

NumericsQueryLayer(store): getMetric(window: MetricWindow, primitive: NumericPrimitive): NumericResult. Pull rows via store.getObservations(window.metric, window.fromIso, window.toIso). If rows.length === 0 => { primitive, value: null, unit: window.unit ?? '', sampleCount: 0 }. UNIT CHECK: collect distinct units across rows; if >1 distinct unit => abstain ({ value:null, sampleCount:0 }) or throw a documented 'cross-unit' refusal — pick abstain to match the no-throw contract, but make it observable (e.g. a separate reason) and TEST it. Compute the primitive over the numeric values: mean, min, max, latest (max t_start), delta (latest - earliest), slope (least-squares over epoch-ms vs value), percentile (document method; getMetric may take a percentile param or default p50), zscore ((latest-mean)/stddev, sampleCount<2 => abstain). getLabTrend(biomarker): build a LabTrend from store.getLabSeries; unknown biomarker => abstain.

ABSOLUTELY NO eval, Function(), new Function, vm, child_process, or execa anywhere in numerics.ts/health-store.ts. The primitive dispatch is a plain switch over the NumericPrimitive union (exhaustive, never guard).

Tests use a temp dir (mkdtemp) + real better-sqlite3 (no mocks); clean up in afterEach. Assert dedup NEW-row count; each primitive against hand-computed values on a fixture series; empty-window abstain; cross-unit refusal; grep test (read the file source and assert /eval|new Function|child_process|execa|\bvm\b/ is absent).
evaluatorNotes:
sc-4-4: insert 5 distinct observations (upsert => 5), re-insert the same 5 (=> 0), getObservations returns 5.
sc-4-5: fixture series e.g. values [10,20,30,40] at increasing timestamps; assert mean=25, min=10, max=40, latest=40, delta=30, slope>0 (matches least-squares), percentile/zscore against documented formulas.
sc-4-6: getMetric on an empty window => value null, sampleCount 0, no throw; getLabTrend('unknown') abstains.
sc-4-7: insert two observations for metric 'weight' with units 'kg' and 'lb'; getMetric mean => abstain/refuse, NOT a blended number.
sc-4-8: read numerics.ts + health-store.ts source; assert no eval/new Function/child_process/execa/vm.
Confirm better-sqlite3 sync usage (no await on store methods). Full suite + build + typecheck green.
